title: Robust Filipino Gobo Burdock Dinuguan
description: A traditional Filipino dish featuring gobo burdock, dashi extract, and a crumbly texture, served on a cedar plank. This herbal meal prep dish is onion and garlic free.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced gobo burdock and cook until it is tender and lightly browned.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the dashi extract, pork blood, vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cooked gobo burdock to the bowl and stir until it is well coated with the sauce.
Transfer the mixture to a cedar plank and shape into a crumbly texture.
Bake the dinuguan in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
Remove the dinuguan from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
